steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage describes the plover and crocodile as having a symbiotic relationship where each gains a benefit.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean mutually beneficial — the plover gets food, the crocodile gets bacteria removed; both gain something. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Reciprocal: "This reciprocal relationship" — reciprocal means each party gives and receives in return, exactly describing the plover-crocodile exchange. ✓
  • Choice B — Interchangeable: "This interchangeable relationship" — interchangeable means the two parties could be swapped; a bird and a crocodile cannot substitute for each other. ✗
  • Choice C — Inefficient: "This inefficient relationship" — inefficient means wasteful or poorly functioning; the relationship clearly works well for both parties. ✗
  • Choice D — Unequal: "This unequal relationship" — unequal implies one side benefits more; the passage presents both parties as gaining from the arrangement. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
